# Dinner Spinner

> A playful meal picker for couples and families. Spin three reels — cuisine,
> style, and protein — or filter by time and dietary needs to get curated
> recipe suggestions. The catalogue covers Bengali, Indian, Chinese, Asian,
> Continental, Mexican and Mediterranean cuisines.

This file follows the [llms.txt](https://llmstxt.org/) convention: a
machine-readable index of the site's primary content for LLMs and AI agents.

- Site: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/
- Browse: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/
- Sitemap: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/sitemap.xml
- Full content (all recipes inlined as markdown): https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/llms-full.txt

## How recipes are organised

Each recipe has: name (often plus a localised name), description, cuisine,
style (curry, stir-fry, grill, …), protein base, meal types (breakfast,
lunch, dinner, snacks), prep + cook times, difficulty, servings, spice
level (1–5), dietary flags (vegetarian, vegan, non-veg, egg, gluten-free,
dairy-free), ingredients with quantities, numbered steps, and optional tips.


## Macher Jhol (মাছের ঝোল)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/macher-jhol/

A light and aromatic Bengali fish curry cooked with potatoes and nigella seeds in a turmeric-laced broth.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 40 min total (15 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g Rohu fish pieces
- 2 medium potatoes, quartered
- 1 tsp turmeric powder
- 1 tsp nigella seeds (kalonji)
- 2 green chillies, slit
- 1 tbsp mustard oil
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 bay leaf
- Salt to taste
- 2 cups water

**Steps:**
1. Marinate fish with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
2. Heat mustard oil in a kadai until smoking, then reduce heat.
3. Fry the fish pieces lightly on both sides and set aside.
4. In the same oil, temper nigella seeds, cumin, and bay leaf.
5. Add potatoes and fry for 3-4 minutes. Add turmeric and green chillies.
6. Pour in water, bring to a boil, and simmer until potatoes are tender.
7. Gently add fish pieces and cook for 5-7 minutes on low heat.
8. Serve hot with steamed rice.

**Tips:**
- Use mustard oil for authentic flavour.
- Do not stir too much or the fish will break.

---

## Chingri Malai Curry (চিংড়ি মালাই কারি)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chingri-malai-curry/

Succulent prawns simmered in a rich, creamy coconut milk gravy — a quintessential Bengali celebration dish.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Prawn
**Time:** 45 min total (20 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g prawns, cleaned and deveined
- 1 cup coconut milk
- 1 large onion, paste
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 2 green cardamom
- 1 small cinnamon stick
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate prawns with turmeric and salt.
2. Heat mustard oil, lightly fry prawns and set aside.
3. In the same oil, temper bay leaves, cardamom, and cinnamon.
4. Add onion paste and cook until golden.
5. Add ginger paste, turmeric, and sugar. Cook 2 minutes.
6. Pour in coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
7. Add prawns and cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes.
8. Serve with steamed Gobindobhog rice.

**Tips:**
- Do not boil coconut milk vigorously or it will split.

---

## Kosha Mangsho (কষা মাংস)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/kosha-mangsho/

Slow-cooked Bengali mutton curry with a deeply spiced, reduced onion-based gravy that clings to every piece.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 80 min total (20 prep + 60 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g mutton on the bone
- 3 large onions, sliced
- 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 3 tbsp mustard oil
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 4 green cardamom
- 4 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 cup yogurt
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate mutton with yogurt, turmeric, and salt for 30 minutes.
2. Heat mustard oil and fry onions until deep golden brown.
3. Add ginger-garlic paste and whole spices, sauté 2 minutes.
4. Add marinated mutton and sear on high heat for 5 minutes.
5. Add red chilli powder and a splash of water.
6. Cover and slow-cook on low heat for 50-60 minutes until tender.
7. Uncover and cook on high to reduce gravy until it coats the meat.
8. Finish with garam masala. Serve with luchi or paratha.

**Tips:**
- The key is patience — slow cooking makes the meat melt-in-mouth.
- Use bone-in mutton for the best flavour.

---

## Shorshe Ilish (সর্ষে ইলিশ)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/shorshe-ilish/

Hilsa fish steamed in a pungent mustard paste sauce — the crown jewel of Bengali cuisine.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 4 pieces Hilsa fish
- 3 tbsp mustard seeds, ground to paste
- 1 tsp nigella seeds
- 4 green chillies
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 3 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste
- 1/2 cup water

**Steps:**
1. Marinate hilsa with turmeric and salt for 10 minutes.
2. Mix mustard paste with mustard oil, green chillies, turmeric, and salt.
3. Heat mustard oil, temper nigella seeds.
4. Lightly fry the fish on both sides.
5. Pour the mustard sauce over the fish.
6. Add water, cover and steam-cook for 12-15 minutes on low.
7. Serve with hot steamed rice.

**Tips:**
- Use yellow and black mustard seeds together for best flavour.
- Keep the heat low while steaming to avoid breaking the fish.

---

## Aloo Posto (আলু পোস্ত)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/aloo-posto/

Potatoes cooked in a poppy seed paste with minimal spices — a beloved everyday Bengali comfort dish.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 3 medium potatoes, cubed
- 3 tbsp poppy seeds, soaked and ground
- 1 tsp nigella seeds
- 2 green chillies
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Soak poppy seeds in warm water for 30 minutes and grind to a paste.
2. Heat mustard oil, temper nigella seeds.
3. Add potato cubes and turmeric, fry for 3-4 minutes.
4. Add poppy seed paste, green chillies, and salt.
5. Add a splash of water, cover and cook until potatoes are done.
6. Serve as a side with rice and dal.

**Tips:**
- Soak poppy seeds adequately for a smooth paste.

---

## Begun Bhaja (বেগুন ভাজা)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/begun-bhaja/

Thinly sliced eggplant, coated in turmeric and salt, shallow-fried until crispy golden — a Bengali staple side.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 15 min total (5 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 large eggplant, sliced into rounds
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- Mustard oil for frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Slice eggplant into thin rounds.
2. Rub with turmeric, chilli powder, and salt.
3. Heat mustard oil in a flat pan.
4. Shallow fry slices until golden and crispy on both sides.
5. Drain on paper towels and serve hot with rice.

---

## Luchi-Alur Dom (লুচি-আলুর দম)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/luchi-alur-dom/

Fluffy deep-fried white flour bread paired with a spicy, tangy potato curry — a classic Bengali breakfast or feast dish.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 50 min total (20 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** breakfast, lunch · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups maida (all-purpose flour)
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 4 medium potatoes, halved
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 1 tomato, pureed
- Oil for deep frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Knead maida with ghee and water into a soft dough. Rest 20 minutes.
2. Boil potatoes until just tender, then halve them.
3. Heat oil, temper cumin and bay leaves.
4. Add ginger paste, tomato puree, and spices. Cook until oil separates.
5. Add potatoes, coat well, and simmer for 10 minutes.
6. Roll small dough balls into thin discs.
7. Deep fry luchis until they puff up golden.
8. Serve hot luchis with alur dom.

**Tips:**
- Knead the dough well for soft, puffy luchis.

---

## Mishti Doi (মিষ্টি দই)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/mishti-doi/

Sweetened, caramelized yogurt set in earthen pots — a silky Bengali dessert with deep jaggery notes.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 385 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 litre full-fat milk
- 100g jaggery or sugar
- 2 tbsp yogurt (as starter)
- A pinch of cardamom powder

**Steps:**
1. Boil milk and reduce to about 3/4 volume.
2. Melt jaggery separately and strain.
3. Mix jaggery into warm milk. Let it cool to lukewarm.
4. Whisk in yogurt starter and cardamom.
5. Pour into earthen pots or bowls.
6. Cover and set in a warm place for 6-8 hours.
7. Refrigerate until chilled. Serve cold.

**Tips:**
- Earthen pots give the best texture and flavour.

---

## Rasgulla (রসগোল্লা)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/rasgulla/

Soft, spongy cottage cheese balls soaked in light sugar syrup — an iconic Bengali sweet.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 50 min total (30 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 litre milk
- 2 tbsp lemon juice
- 1.5 cups sugar
- 4 cups water
- 1 tsp rose water
- 2 green cardamom pods

**Steps:**
1. Boil milk, add lemon juice to curdle. Strain through muslin cloth.
2. Wash chenna under cold water to remove sourness.
3. Knead chenna for 8-10 minutes until smooth and crack-free.
4. Roll into small, smooth balls.
5. Prepare sugar syrup by boiling sugar with water and cardamom.
6. Drop chenna balls into boiling syrup. Cover and cook 15 minutes.
7. They will double in size. Let them cool in syrup.
8. Chill and serve cold.

**Tips:**
- Knead until absolutely smooth — cracks mean they will break in syrup.

---

## Sandesh (সন্দেশ)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/sandesh/

Delicate Bengali sweet made from fresh cottage cheese and sugar, flavoured with cardamom or saffron.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 30 min total (20 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 litre milk
- 2 tbsp lemon juice
- 1/3 cup powdered sugar
- 1/4 tsp cardamom powder
- A few saffron strands
- Pistachios for garnish

**Steps:**
1. Curdle milk with lemon juice and drain whey.
2. Knead chenna until smooth.
3. Cook chenna and sugar in a non-stick pan on low heat for 5-7 minutes.
4. Add cardamom and saffron, mix well.
5. When the mixture leaves the sides of the pan, remove from heat.
6. Shape into moulds or small rounds.
7. Garnish with pistachios. Serve at room temperature or chilled.

---

## Bengali Fish Fry (মাছ ভাজা)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/bengali-fish-fry/

Crumb-coated, spiced fish cutlets shallow-fried to a crunchy golden perfection — a Kolkata street-food legend.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 35 min total (20 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 4 bhetki or any firm fish fillets
- 1 egg, beaten
- 1 cup breadcrumbs
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp garlic paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- Oil for frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate fish with ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, chilli powder, lemon juice, and salt.
2. Let it rest for 30 minutes.
3. Dip each fillet in beaten egg.
4. Coat evenly with breadcrumbs.
5. Shallow fry in hot oil until golden on both sides.
6. Drain and serve hot with kasundi (mustard sauce).

**Tips:**
- Press breadcrumbs firmly for a crisp coating.

---

## Doi Maach (দই মাছ)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/doi-maach/

Fish simmered in a creamy, mildly spiced yogurt gravy — a rich Bengali classic served at celebrations.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 40 min total (15 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g Rohu fish pieces
- 1 cup yogurt, whisked
- 1 large onion, paste
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 green cardamom
- 2 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate fish with turmeric and salt.
2. Fry fish lightly in mustard oil and set aside.
3. Temper whole spices in the same oil.
4. Add onion paste and cook until golden.
5. Add ginger paste, sugar, and whisked yogurt. Stir continuously.
6. Simmer for 5 minutes, then gently add fish.
7. Cook covered on low for 10-12 minutes.
8. Serve with fragrant Gobindobhog rice.

---

## Mochar Ghonto (মোচার ঘণ্ট)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/mochar-ghonto/

Banana blossom cooked with coconut, spices and sometimes prawns — a unique and cherished Bengali preparation.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 55 min total (25 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 banana blossom (mocha)
- 2 potatoes, diced small
- 2 tbsp grated coconut
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 bay leaves
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 2 green chillies
- Salt to taste
- 1 tsp sugar

**Steps:**
1. Clean banana blossom, remove outer petals, chop finely and soak in water.
2. Boil or steam the chopped mocha until soft. Drain.
3. Heat mustard oil, temper cumin and bay leaves.
4. Add potatoes and fry until light golden.
5. Add the cooked mocha, turmeric, and salt. Stir well.
6. Cook on medium heat for 10-12 minutes.
7. Add grated coconut, garam masala, and sugar. Mix well.
8. Cook 5 more minutes until dry. Serve with rice.

**Tips:**
- Rub oil on hands while cleaning banana blossom to prevent staining.

---

## Cholar Dal (ছোলার ডাল)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/cholar-dal/

Bengal gram lentils cooked with coconut and raisins, sweetly spiced — a festive Bengali dal like no other.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 40 min total (10 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup chana dal, soaked
- 2 tbsp grated coconut
- 1 tbsp raisins
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 green cardamom
- 1 small cinnamon stick
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 tbsp ghee
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook soaked chana dal with turmeric and salt until soft.
2. Heat ghee, temper cumin, bay leaf, cardamom, and cinnamon.
3. Add cooked dal and stir well.
4. Add coconut, raisins, and sugar.
5. Simmer for 10 minutes until flavours meld.
6. Serve with luchi or rice.

---

## Dhokar Dalna (ধোকার ডালনা)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/dhokar-dalna/

Fried lentil cakes in a spicy tomato-based gravy — a unique Bengali vegetarian dish bursting with flavour.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 60 min total (30 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup chana dal, soaked 4 hours
- 1 tsp cumin paste
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp sugar
- 3 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Grind soaked chana dal to a coarse paste with ginger and cumin.
2. Steam or microwave the paste until set firm. Cut into diamond shapes.
3. Shallow fry the dhoka pieces until golden on both sides.
4. In another pan, heat oil, temper cumin and bay leaves.
5. Add tomato puree, turmeric, chilli powder, and sugar. Cook 5 minutes.
6. Add water to make a medium gravy.
7. Add fried dhokas and simmer 10 minutes.
8. Serve hot with steamed rice.

---

## Shukto (শুক্তো)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/shukto/

A bitter-sweet medley of vegetables in a milk-based gravy — a traditional Bengali meal starter.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 45 min total (20 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 small bitter gourd, sliced
- 1 drumstick, cut into pieces
- 1 raw banana, sliced
- 1 sweet potato, cubed
- 1 cup milk
- 1 tsp panch phoron
- 1 tbsp mustard paste
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 1 bay leaf
- Salt to taste
- 1 tsp sugar

**Steps:**
1. Fry bitter gourd slices in mustard oil until crisp. Set aside.
2. In same oil, temper panch phoron and bay leaf.
3. Add all vegetables and fry for 3 minutes.
4. Add ginger paste, turmeric, salt, and water. Cook until tender.
5. Add milk and mustard paste, stir gently.
6. Simmer for 5 minutes. Add sugar.
7. Top with fried bitter gourd and serve as the first course with rice.

**Tips:**
- Shukto is traditionally the first dish served in a Bengali meal.

---

## Posto Bora (পোস্ত বড়া)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/posto-bora/

Deep-fried poppy seed fritters with onion and green chillies — a crispy, addictive Bengali snack.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (15 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** snacks, lunch · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 4 tbsp poppy seeds, soaked and ground
- 1 small onion, finely chopped
- 2 green chillies, chopped
- Salt to taste
- Mustard oil for frying

**Steps:**
1. Soak poppy seeds for 1 hour and grind to a thick paste.
2. Mix with chopped onion, green chillies, and salt.
3. Heat mustard oil in a kadai.
4. Drop spoonfuls of the mixture into hot oil.
5. Fry until golden brown on all sides.
6. Drain and serve hot with rice.

---

## Jhinge Posto (ঝিঙে পোস্ত)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/jhinge-posto/

Ridge gourd cooked with poppy seed paste — a light, everyday Bengali vegetable dish.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 ridge gourds, peeled and sliced
- 2 tbsp poppy seeds, ground to paste
- 1 tsp nigella seeds
- 1 green chilli
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 1/4 tsp turmeric
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Peel and slice ridge gourd.
2. Heat mustard oil, temper nigella seeds.
3. Add ridge gourd slices with turmeric and salt.
4. Cook covered for 5-7 minutes until soft.
5. Add poppy seed paste and green chilli.
6. Cook for 5 more minutes, stirring occasionally. Serve with rice.

---

## Aloor Dum (আলুর দম)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/aloor-dum/

Baby potatoes slow-cooked in a rich, spicy tomato gravy — a Bengali comfort classic.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 35 min total (10 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** breakfast, lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g baby potatoes, boiled and peeled
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 bay leaves
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Boil and peel baby potatoes. Prick with a fork.
2. Heat mustard oil, fry potatoes until lightly golden. Set aside.
3. Temper cumin and bay leaves in the same oil.
4. Add ginger paste and tomato puree. Cook 5 minutes.
5. Add spices, sugar, salt, and a cup of water.
6. Add potatoes, cover and simmer 15 minutes.
7. Serve with luchi, paratha, or rice.

---

## Panta Bhat (পান্তা ভাত)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/panta-bhat/

Fermented rice soaked overnight in water, served with fried hilsa, green chilli, and onion — a rustic Bengali classic.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 15 min total (5 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, lunch · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups leftover cooked rice
- Water to soak
- 1 piece fried hilsa or any fish
- 1 raw onion, sliced
- 2 green chillies
- 1 tsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Soak leftover rice in water overnight at room temperature.
2. In the morning, drain some water.
3. Add salt and mustard oil.
4. Serve with fried fish, sliced onion, and green chillies.

**Tips:**
- This is a traditional Poila Boishakh (Bengali New Year) dish.

---

## Keema Doi (কিমার দই)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/keema-doi/

Minced mutton cooked in a tangy yogurt gravy with warming spices — a hearty Bengali keema preparation.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 50 min total (15 prep + 35 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g mutton mince
- 1 cup yogurt
- 1 large onion, sliced
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp garlic paste
- 2 green cardamom
- 2 cloves
- 1 bay leaf
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat mustard oil, fry onions until deep golden.
2. Add ginger-garlic paste and whole spices, sauté.
3. Add mutton mince, break up and cook until browned.
4. Add turmeric, salt, and cook for 5 minutes.
5. Whisk yogurt and add slowly, stirring continuously.
6. Cover and cook on low for 20-25 minutes.
7. Finish with garam masala. Serve with rice or paratha.

---

## Chicken Kosha (চিকেন কষা)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-kosha/

Bengali-style slow-cooked chicken in a thick, deeply spiced onion and tomato gravy.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 55 min total (15 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g chicken pieces
- 3 onions, sliced
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp cumin powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 3 tbsp mustard oil
- 2 bay leaves
- 3 green cardamom
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat mustard oil, fry onions until deep brown.
2. Add ginger-garlic paste and fry for 2 minutes.
3. Add tomatoes and cook until mushy.
4. Add all dry spices and cook for 2 minutes.
5. Add chicken pieces, sear on high heat.
6. Add a splash of water, cover and cook on low for 30 minutes.
7. Uncover and cook on high to reduce the gravy.
8. Finish with garam masala. Serve with rice or roti.

---

## Fulkopir Singara (ফুলকপির সিঙাড়া)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/fulkopir-singara/

Crispy, flaky pastry pockets stuffed with spiced cauliflower and peas — Kolkata s iconic teatime snack.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 50 min total (30 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** snacks, breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups maida
- 1/4 cup oil (for dough)
- 1 small cauliflower, finely chopped
- 1/2 cup peas
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 green chilli, chopped
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- Oil for deep frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Make dough with maida, oil, and water. Rest for 30 minutes.
2. Cook cauliflower filling with spices until dry and cooled.
3. Roll dough into circles, cut in half.
4. Form cones, stuff with filling, seal edges with water.
5. Deep fry on medium-low heat until golden and flaky.
6. Serve hot with tamarind chutney or kashundi.

**Tips:**
- Fry on low heat for even cooking and flaky layers.

---

## Telebhaja (Beguni & Peyaji) (তেলেভাজা (বেগুনি ও পেঁয়াজি))

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/telebhaja/

Crispy batter-fried eggplant slices and onion fritters — the quintessential rainy-day Bengali telebhaja platter.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 30 min total (15 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 large eggplant, sliced
- 2 large onions, sliced into rings
- 1 cup besan (gram flour)
- 1/4 cup rice flour
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp nigella seeds
- Water to make batter
- Mustard oil for frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Make a batter with besan, rice flour, turmeric, chilli, nigella seeds, and salt.
2. Slice eggplant into rounds and onions into rings.
3. Heat mustard oil for deep frying.
4. Dip eggplant slices in batter and fry until golden (beguni).
5. Mix remaining onion rings into batter, drop spoonfuls into oil (peyaji).
6. Fry until crispy. Drain and serve hot with chai.

**Tips:**
- Adding rice flour makes the fritters extra crispy.

---

## Kolkata Biryani (কলকাতা বিরিয়ানি)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/kolkata-biryani/

Fragrant layered rice with spiced mutton, potatoes, and eggs — Kolkata s unique take on the royal biryani.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 100 min total (40 prep + 60 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 6 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g mutton on the bone
- 3 cups basmati rice
- 1 cup yogurt
- 3 large onions, sliced
- 2 potatoes, halved
- 4 boiled eggs
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp biryani masala
- Saffron soaked in warm milk
- 2 tbsp ghee
- Whole spices (cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, bay leaf)
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate mutton with yogurt and spices for 2 hours.
2. Fry onions until deep golden. Reserve half for layering.
3. Cook mutton with fried onions, ginger-garlic paste, and spices until tender.
4. Parboil rice with whole spices until 70% cooked.
5. Fry potatoes and boiled eggs until golden.
6. Layer mutton, rice, fried onions, saffron milk, potatoes, and eggs in a heavy pot.
7. Seal with dough or foil. Cook on very low heat (dum) for 25 minutes.
8. Gently mix while serving. Accompany with raita.

**Tips:**
- The potato soaking up the spices is the hallmark of Kolkata biryani.
- Use a very heavy-bottomed pot for even dum cooking.

---

## Lau Ghonto (লাউ ঘণ্ট)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/lau-ghonto/

Bottle gourd cooked with tiny prawns and spices — a homestyle Bengali dish that is pure comfort.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 medium bottle gourd, peeled and cubed
- 1 tsp panch phoron
- 1 bay leaf
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp sugar
- 1 tsp grated coconut
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 1 green chilli
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Peel and cube the bottle gourd.
2. Heat mustard oil, temper panch phoron and bay leaf.
3. Add bottle gourd, turmeric, and salt.
4. Cover and cook on low heat until soft (gourd releases its own water).
5. Add sugar and grated coconut.
6. Cook until slightly dry. Serve with rice and dal.

---

## Muri Ghonto (মুড়ি ঘণ্ট)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/muri-ghonto/

A unique Bengali dish of rice cooked with fish head and spices — deeply flavourful and traditional.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 45 min total (15 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 large Rohu fish head
- 1 cup Gobindobhog or short-grain rice, soaked
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 1 bay leaf
- 2 green cardamom
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 3 tbsp mustard oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Fry the fish head in mustard oil until golden. Break into pieces.
2. In the same oil, temper cumin, bay leaf, and cardamom.
3. Add soaked rice and fry for 2 minutes.
4. Add turmeric, ginger paste, salt, and sugar.
5. Add water (1.5x rice volume) and the fish head pieces.
6. Cook covered on low heat until rice is done and dry.
7. Finish with ghee. Serve as a standalone dish.

**Tips:**
- The fish head adds incredible richness to the rice.

---

## Prawn Malai Curry (চিংড়ি মালাইকারি)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/prawn-malai-curry/

Juicy prawns bathed in a velvety coconut cream sauce with subtle spices — luxurious and aromatic.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Prawn
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g large prawns
- 1 cup coconut cream
- 1 onion, paste
- 1 tsp ginger paste
- 1/4 tsp turmeric
- 2 green cardamom
- 1 small cinnamon stick
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 tbsp mustard oil
- 2 green chillies
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Clean and devein prawns. Marinate with turmeric and salt.
2. Heat mustard oil, fry prawns briefly, set aside.
3. Temper cardamom and cinnamon in the oil.
4. Add onion paste and cook until light golden.
5. Add ginger paste, green chillies, and sugar.
6. Pour in coconut cream and simmer gently.
7. Add prawns and cook 5-7 minutes on low heat.
8. Serve with steamed basmati or Gobindobhog rice.

---

## Chholar Dal (ছোলার ডাল)

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chholar-dal/

Sweetly spiced Bengal gram dal with coconut — the perfect accompaniment to luchi for any Bengali celebration.

**Cuisine:** Bengali (West Bengal) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 35 min total (10 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup chana dal
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 bay leaves
- 2 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 3 tbsp grated coconut
- 1 tbsp raisins
- 1 tsp sugar
- 1/4 tsp turmeric
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook chana dal with turmeric until soft but not mushy.
2. Heat ghee, temper cumin, bay leaves, cloves, and cinnamon.
3. Add cooked dal and mix.
4. Add coconut, raisins, and sugar.
5. Simmer for 8-10 minutes.
6. Serve with luchi for a festive meal.

---

## Butter Chicken

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/butter-chicken/

Tender chicken pieces in a luscious, creamy tomato-butter gravy — the undisputed king of North Indian curries.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Punjab) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 50 min total (20 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken, boneless cubes
- 1 cup tomato puree
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1/2 cup cream
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 tsp kasuri methi
- 1 tsp sugar
- 2 green cardamom
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate chicken with yogurt and spices for 1 hour.
2. Grill or pan-fry chicken until charred. Set aside.
3. Melt butter, add cardamom, then tomato puree.
4. Cook tomatoes for 10 minutes until thick.
5. Add spices, sugar, and kasuri methi.
6. Add cream and mix well.
7. Add chicken pieces and simmer 10 minutes.
8. Finish with extra butter. Serve with naan or rice.

---

## Dal Makhani

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/dal-makhani/

Black lentils and kidney beans slow-simmered overnight with butter and cream — rich, smoky, and indulgent.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Punjab) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 75 min total (15 prep + 60 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup whole black urad dal, soaked overnight
- 1/4 cup rajma, soaked overnight
- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp cream
- 1 large onion, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 tsp kasuri methi
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook soaked dal and rajma until very soft.
2. Heat butter, sauté onions until golden.
3. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato puree.
4. Cook until oil separates.
5. Add cooked dal, spices, and simmer on low for 30-40 minutes.
6. Add cream and kasuri methi.
7. Serve with naan or rice, topped with a dollop of butter.

**Tips:**
- The longer you simmer, the creamier and better it gets.

---

## Rajma

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/rajma/

Kidney beans in a thick, hearty tomato-onion gravy — the ultimate North Indian comfort food with rice.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Punjab) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 50 min total (10 prep + 40 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups rajma, soaked overnight
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tsp coriander powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste
- Coriander leaves

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook soaked rajma until soft.
2. Heat oil, temper cumin seeds.
3. Fry onions until golden, add ginger-garlic paste.
4. Add tomato puree and spices. Cook until oil separates.
5. Add cooked rajma with its liquid.
6. Simmer for 20 minutes until thick.
7. Garnish with coriander. Serve with steamed rice.

---

## Palak Paneer

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/palak-paneer/

Cubes of paneer in a vibrant, silky spinach gravy — a nutritious and much-loved vegetarian classic.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (North India) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 250g paneer, cubed
- 4 cups spinach, blanched
- 1 onion, chopped
- 2 green chillies
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tbsp cream
- 1 tbsp butter
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Blanch spinach in boiling water, then ice bath. Blend to a smooth puree.
2. Heat butter, temper cumin seeds.
3. Sauté onion and ginger-garlic paste until golden.
4. Add spinach puree, green chillies, and salt. Cook 5 minutes.
5. Add paneer cubes and garam masala.
6. Simmer for 5 minutes. Finish with cream.
7. Serve with roti or naan.

---

## Paneer Tikka

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/paneer-tikka/

Marinated paneer and vegetables grilled or baked to smoky perfection — a popular vegetarian appetiser.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Punjab) · **Style:** Grilled · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 35 min total (20 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** snacks, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 250g paneer, cubed
- 1 bell pepper, cubed
- 1 onion, cubed
- 1/2 cup thick yogurt
- 1 tbsp tikka masala
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste
- Chaat masala for garnish

**Steps:**
1. Mix yogurt with all spices, oil, and lemon juice.
2. Marinate paneer and vegetables for 1 hour.
3. Thread onto skewers.
4. Grill in oven at 220°C or on a tandoor for 12-15 minutes.
5. Sprinkle chaat masala and lemon juice.
6. Serve with mint chutney.

---

## Rogan Josh

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/rogan-josh/

A rich Kashmiri mutton curry with deep red colour from Kashmiri chillies and aromatic whole spices.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Kashmir) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Mutton
**Time:** 90 min total (20 prep + 70 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 750g mutton pieces
- 1 cup yogurt
- 2 tbsp Kashmiri red chilli powder
- 1 tsp fennel powder
- 1 tsp dry ginger powder
- 4 green cardamom
- 2 black cardamom
- 4 cloves
- 1 cinnamon stick
- 2 bay leaves
- 3 tbsp oil or ghee
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat oil, add whole spices and bay leaves.
2. Add mutton pieces and sear on high heat.
3. Add Kashmiri chilli powder and dry ginger. Stir well.
4. Add yogurt gradually, stirring continuously.
5. Add fennel powder and salt.
6. Add water, cover and slow-cook for 60-70 minutes.
7. Cook until oil rises to the top and gravy thickens.
8. Serve with steamed rice or naan.

---

## Kadai Paneer

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/kadai-paneer/

Paneer tossed with bell peppers in a chunky tomato gravy spiced with crushed coriander and dried red chillies.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (North India) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 250g paneer, cubed
- 1 bell pepper, cubed
- 3 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 tbsp crushed coriander seeds
- 3 dried red chillies
- 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp kasuri methi
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Dry roast coriander seeds and dried chillies, crush coarsely.
2. Heat oil, sauté onions until translucent.
3. Add ginger-garlic paste, then tomato puree.
4. Cook until thick and oil separates.
5. Add crushed spice mix and salt.
6. Add paneer and bell pepper, toss well.
7. Cook 5 minutes. Finish with kasuri methi.
8. Serve hot with roti or naan.

---

## Baingan Bharta

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/baingan-bharta/

Fire-roasted and mashed eggplant cooked with tomatoes, onions, and bold spices — smoky and deeply satisfying.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Punjab) · **Style:** Dry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 large eggplants
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 2 green chillies, chopped
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1/2 tsp garam masala
- Fresh coriander
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Roast eggplants directly over flame until charred and soft.
2. Peel and mash the roasted flesh.
3. Heat oil, temper cumin seeds.
4. Fry onions until golden, add ginger-garlic paste.
5. Add tomatoes, green chillies, and spices. Cook 5 minutes.
6. Add mashed eggplant and mix well.
7. Cook for 10 minutes. Garnish with coriander.
8. Serve with roti or paratha.

---

## Tandoori Chicken

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/tandoori-chicken/

Chicken marinated in yogurt and spices, cooked at high heat until smoky and charred — an iconic Indian dish.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Punjab) · **Style:** Grilled · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 45 min total (15 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 kg chicken, scored
- 1 cup thick yogurt
- 2 tbsp tandoori masala
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tbsp oil
- 1 tsp garam masala
- Red food colour (optional)
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Score chicken and apply lemon juice and salt. Rest 15 minutes.
2. Mix yogurt with all spices and oil.
3. Coat chicken thoroughly. Marinate for 4-6 hours.
4. Preheat oven to 230°C.
5. Place on a wire rack over a baking tray.
6. Bake for 25-30 minutes, flipping once.
7. Serve with onion rings, lemon wedges, and mint chutney.

---

## Shahi Paneer

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/shahi-paneer/

Paneer cubes in a rich, creamy cashew and tomato gravy fit for royalty — indulgent and aromatic.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (North India) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 250g paneer, cubed
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 10 cashews, soaked and ground
- 1 onion, paste
- 1 tbsp cream
- 1 tsp ginger-garlic paste
- 1/4 tsp turmeric
- 1 tsp garam masala
- A few saffron strands
- 2 tbsp ghee
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat ghee, sauté onion paste until golden.
2. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomato puree. Cook 8 minutes.
3. Add cashew paste, turmeric, and saffron.
4. Add water to adjust consistency, simmer 5 minutes.
5. Add paneer cubes and garam masala.
6. Finish with cream. Serve with naan.

---

## Malai Kofta

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/malai-kofta/

Soft paneer and potato dumplings in a velvety, mildly spiced tomato-cream gravy — pure indulgence.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (North India) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 55 min total (30 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** hard · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 200g paneer, grated
- 2 boiled potatoes, mashed
- 2 tbsp cornflour
- 10 cashews, ground
- 2 tomatoes, pureed
- 1 onion, paste
- 1/2 cup cream
- 1 tsp garam masala
- 1 tsp kasuri methi
- 2 tbsp butter
- Oil for frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Mix grated paneer, mashed potato, cornflour, and salt. Shape into balls.
2. Deep fry koftas until golden. Set aside.
3. Heat butter, sauté onion paste until golden.
4. Add tomato puree and cook until thick.
5. Add cashew paste, garam masala, and kasuri methi.
6. Add water and cream, simmer 5 minutes.
7. Place koftas in gravy just before serving.
8. Serve with naan or pulao.

**Tips:**
- Add koftas to gravy just before serving so they stay intact.

---

## Pav Bhaji

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/pav-bhaji/

A spicy mash of mixed vegetables served with buttery, toasted pav buns — Mumbai s favourite street food.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (Maharashtra) · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 40 min total (15 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 3 potatoes, boiled
- 1 cup cauliflower, boiled
- 1/2 cup peas, boiled
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 bell pepper, chopped
- 2 tbsp pav bhaji masala
- 3 tbsp butter
- 8 pav buns
- Lemon juice
- Salt to taste
- Coriander leaves

**Steps:**
1. Boil and mash all vegetables coarsely.
2. Heat butter, sauté onions and bell pepper.
3. Add tomatoes, pav bhaji masala, and cook until soft.
4. Add all mashed vegetables, mix and mash further on heat.
5. Add water for desired consistency, simmer 10 minutes.
6. Slit and toast pav buns with butter.
7. Serve bhaji topped with butter, onion, and lemon.

---

## Dal Tadka

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/dal-tadka/

Yellow lentils tempered with cumin, garlic, and ghee — an everyday Indian comfort dal served in every home.

**Cuisine:** North Indian (North India) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 35 min total (10 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup toor dal
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 2 tbsp ghee
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 4 cloves garlic, sliced
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 tomato, chopped
- 2 dried red chillies
- 1 tsp red chilli powder
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Pressure cook toor dal with turmeric and salt until soft.
2. Mash dal and adjust consistency with water.
3. Heat ghee, add cumin seeds and dried red chillies.
4. Add sliced garlic and fry until golden.
5. Add onion and tomato, cook 3 minutes.
6. Pour the sizzling tadka over the dal.
7. Garnish with coriander. Serve with rice or roti.

---

## Masala Dosa

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/masala-dosa/

Crispy fermented rice-lentil crepe filled with spiced potato masala — South India s most iconic breakfast.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Karnataka) · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 40 min total (20 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups dosa batter (fermented)
- 3 potatoes, boiled and mashed
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp urad dal
- 8-10 curry leaves
- 2 green chillies
- 1/2 tsp turmeric
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. For filling: heat oil, temper mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
2. Add onions, green chillies, and sauté until soft.
3. Add turmeric, salt, and mashed potatoes. Mix well.
4. Heat a dosa tawa, pour a ladle of batter and spread thin.
5. Drizzle oil around edges, cook until crispy and golden.
6. Place potato filling in the center, fold the dosa.
7. Serve with coconut chutney and sambar.

---

## Idli Sambar

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/idli-sambar/

Soft, fluffy steamed rice cakes served with a tangy lentil-vegetable stew — a wholesome South Indian meal.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Tamil Nadu) · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups idli batter (fermented)
- 1/2 cup toor dal
- 1 drumstick, cut
- 1 carrot, diced
- 1 tomato, chopped
- 1 tbsp sambar powder
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp tamarind paste
- Curry leaves
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Steam idli batter in moulds for 10-12 minutes.
2. Cook toor dal until soft.
3. Heat oil, temper mustard seeds and curry leaves.
4. Add vegetables and sauté. Add sambar powder and tomato.
5. Add cooked dal, tamarind, salt, and water.
6. Simmer for 15 minutes.
7. Serve idlis with hot sambar and coconut chutney.

---

## Upma

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/upma/

Savoury semolina porridge tempered with mustard seeds, curry leaves, and vegetables — a quick South Indian breakfast.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (South India) · **Style:** Savory · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 20 min total (5 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup semolina (rava)
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 green chilli, chopped
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp urad dal
- 8 curry leaves
- 1 carrot, diced
- 2 tbsp peanuts
- 2 tbsp oil
- 2 cups water
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Dry roast semolina until light golden. Set aside.
2. Heat oil, temper mustard seeds, urad dal, and curry leaves.
3. Add peanuts, onion, green chilli, and carrot. Sauté.
4. Add water and salt, bring to a boil.
5. Slowly add roasted semolina while stirring continuously.
6. Cover and cook on low for 2-3 minutes.
7. Serve hot with coconut chutney.

---

## Chicken Chettinad

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-chettinad/

Fiery South Indian chicken curry with freshly ground spice paste — bold, aromatic, and intensely flavourful.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Tamil Nadu) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 55 min total (20 prep + 35 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 5/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken
- 2 onions, chopped
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
- 2 tsp black pepper
- 1 tsp fennel seeds
- 4 dried red chillies
- 1 tsp poppy seeds
- 1 star anise
- 1/2 cup coconut, grated
- Curry leaves
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Dry roast pepper, fennel, chillies, poppy seeds, and star anise. Grind to paste.
2. Heat oil, add curry leaves and onions. Cook until golden.
3. Add ginger-garlic paste and tomatoes. Cook 5 minutes.
4. Add the ground spice paste and fry 3 minutes.
5. Add chicken pieces and sear well.
6. Add water, cover and cook 25 minutes.
7. Finish with grated coconut. Serve with rice or parotta.

---

## Fish Moilee

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/fish-moilee/

Delicate fish fillets in a light coconut milk curry with curry leaves — a gentle and fragrant Kerala classic.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Kerala) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Fish
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g firm white fish fillets
- 1 cup thin coconut milk
- 1/2 cup thick coconut milk
- 1 onion, sliced
- 1 tomato, sliced
- 6 green chillies, slit
- 1 tsp turmeric
- 10 curry leaves
- 1 tbsp coconut oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat coconut oil, add curry leaves and sliced onions.
2. Sauté until soft, add turmeric and green chillies.
3. Add tomato slices and thin coconut milk.
4. Bring to a simmer, add fish pieces.
5. Cook gently for 10 minutes.
6. Add thick coconut milk and simmer 3 more minutes.
7. Serve with appam or steamed rice.

---

## Medu Vada

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/medu-vada/

Crispy, golden urad dal doughnuts with a fluffy interior — a beloved South Indian breakfast and snack.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (South India) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 30 min total (15 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup urad dal, soaked 4 hours
- 1 small onion, chopped
- 2 green chillies, chopped
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 8 curry leaves, chopped
- A pinch of asafoetida
- Fresh coriander
- Oil for deep frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Grind soaked urad dal to a thick, fluffy batter (minimal water).
2. Mix in onion, chillies, cumin, curry leaves, and salt.
3. Wet your hands, take a portion, shape into a doughnut.
4. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy.
5. Drain and serve with coconut chutney and sambar.

**Tips:**
- The batter should be thick and fluffy — do not add too much water.

---

## Uttapam

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/uttapam/

Thick, soft dosa pancakes topped with onions, tomatoes, and chillies — a quick and filling South Indian meal.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (South India) · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 20 min total (5 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups dosa batter
- 1 onion, chopped
- 1 tomato, chopped
- 2 green chillies, chopped
- Fresh coriander, chopped
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat a dosa tawa, pour batter thick (like a pancake).
2. Sprinkle onion, tomato, chillies, and coriander on top.
3. Press toppings gently into the batter.
4. Drizzle oil around edges, cook on medium heat until golden.
5. Flip and cook the other side briefly.
6. Serve with coconut chutney and sambar.

---

## Pesarattu

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/pesarattu/

Green moong dal crepes from Andhra Pradesh — protein-rich, healthy, and wonderfully crispy.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Andhra Pradesh) · **Style:** Savory · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 cup green moong dal, soaked
- 2 green chillies
- 1 inch ginger
- 1 small onion, chopped
- A pinch of cumin seeds
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Grind soaked moong dal with chillies and ginger to a smooth batter.
2. Add salt and cumin.
3. Heat a tawa, pour batter and spread thin.
4. Sprinkle chopped onion on top.
5. Drizzle oil, cook until crispy and golden.
6. Serve with ginger chutney or upma.

---

## Avial

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/avial/

Mixed vegetables in a thick coconut and yogurt sauce — a creamy, subtly spiced Kerala classic.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Kerala) · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 drumstick, cut
- 1 raw banana, cubed
- 1 carrot, cubed
- 1/2 cup beans, cut
- 1 cup grated coconut
- 2 green chillies
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 1/2 cup yogurt
- 1 tbsp coconut oil
- Curry leaves
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Cook vegetables with turmeric, salt, and minimal water until tender.
2. Grind coconut, cumin, and green chillies to a coarse paste.
3. Add the coconut paste to the vegetables and mix.
4. Simmer for 5 minutes.
5. Remove from heat, stir in yogurt.
6. Drizzle coconut oil and curry leaves on top.
7. Serve with rice.

---

## Rasam Rice

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/rasam-rice/

Peppery, tangy tomato-tamarind soup served over steamed rice — a soothing South Indian comfort meal.

**Cuisine:** South Indian (Tamil Nadu) · **Style:** Soup · **Protein:** Lentils
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1/4 cup toor dal, cooked
- 2 tomatoes, chopped
- 1 tbsp tamarind paste
- 1 tsp rasam powder
- 1 tsp mustard seeds
- 1 tsp cumin seeds
- 2 dried red chillies
- 8 curry leaves
- 2 cloves garlic
- 1 tbsp ghee
- Fresh coriander
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Cook toor dal and mash.
2. Boil tomatoes with tamarind paste and rasam powder.
3. Add cooked dal and water, bring to a boil.
4. Heat ghee, temper mustard, cumin, garlic, chillies, and curry leaves.
5. Pour tempering into the rasam.
6. Garnish with coriander.
7. Pour over hot steamed rice. Serve with papad.

---

## Chicken Manchurian

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chicken-manchurian/

Crispy fried chicken balls tossed in a tangy, spicy soy-chilli sauce — Indo-Chinese comfort at its best.

**Cuisine:** Chinese (Kolkata) · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 40 min total (20 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken mince
- 1 egg
- 3 tbsp cornflour
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp chilli sauce
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 1 onion, diced
- 1 bell pepper, diced
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, minced
- 2 tbsp oil
- Spring onions
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Mix chicken mince with egg, cornflour, and salt. Shape into balls.
2. Deep fry until golden and cooked through. Set aside.
3. Heat oil, sauté garlic and ginger.
4. Add onion and bell pepper, stir fry.
5. Add soy sauce, chilli sauce, vinegar, and a splash of water.
6. Add fried chicken balls and toss well.
7. Garnish with spring onions. Serve with fried rice.

---

## Veg Hakka Noodles

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/veg-hakka-noodles/

Stir-fried noodles with colourful vegetables and soy sauce — a quick Indo-Chinese weeknight favourite.

**Cuisine:** Chinese · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 200g hakka noodles
- 1 carrot, julienned
- 1 bell pepper, julienned
- 1/2 cup cabbage, shredded
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp chilli sauce
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 2 tbsp oil
- Spring onions
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Boil noodles until al dente, drain and toss with oil.
2. Heat oil in a wok on high heat.
3. Add garlic, stir fry 30 seconds.
4. Add all vegetables and stir fry 2-3 minutes.
5. Add soy sauce, chilli sauce, vinegar, and salt.
6. Add noodles and toss vigorously on high heat.
7. Garnish with spring onions. Serve hot.

---

## Chilli Chicken

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chilli-chicken/

Crispy battered chicken tossed with peppers in a hot, sweet, and sour sauce — the Indo-Chinese classic.

**Cuisine:** Chinese · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 500g chicken, boneless cubes
- 2 tbsp cornflour
- 1 egg
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp chilli sauce
- 1 tbsp tomato ketchup
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 1 onion, cubed
- 1 bell pepper, cubed
- 6 dried red chillies
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Marinate chicken with cornflour, egg, salt, and soy sauce.
2. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Set aside.
3. Heat oil in a wok, sauté garlic and dried chillies.
4. Add onion and bell pepper, stir fry on high.
5. Add all sauces, vinegar, and a tbsp of water.
6. Toss in fried chicken and stir fry 2 minutes.
7. Serve as a dry starter or with gravy over noodles.

---

## Veg Fried Rice

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/veg-fried-rice/

Day-old rice stir-fried with vegetables, soy sauce, and a hint of vinegar — fast, satisfying, and versatile.

**Cuisine:** Chinese · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 20 min total (10 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 3 cups cooked rice (day-old, cold)
- 1 carrot, diced
- 1/2 cup peas
- 1/2 cup beans, chopped
- 1/2 cup cabbage, shredded
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 2 tbsp oil
- Spring onions
- White pepper
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Heat oil in a wok on very high heat.
2. Add garlic, stir fry briefly.
3. Add all vegetables, stir fry 2-3 minutes.
4. Add cold rice and break up any clumps.
5. Add soy sauce, vinegar, white pepper, and salt.
6. Toss everything on high heat for 3-4 minutes.
7. Garnish with spring onions. Serve with manchurian or chilli chicken.

---

## Gobi Manchurian

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/gobi-manchurian/

Crispy cauliflower florets drenched in a spicy, sweet, tangy Indo-Chinese sauce — totally addictive.

**Cuisine:** Chinese · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** snacks, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1 medium cauliflower, florets
- 1/2 cup cornflour
- 1/4 cup maida
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp chilli sauce
- 1 tbsp tomato ketchup
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, minced
- 1 bell pepper, diced
- Spring onions
- Oil for frying
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Make batter with cornflour, maida, salt, and water.
2. Dip cauliflower florets in batter and deep fry until golden.
3. Heat oil in a wok, sauté garlic and ginger.
4. Add bell pepper, sauces, vinegar, and water.
5. Add fried cauliflower and toss well.
6. Garnish with spring onions. Serve dry or with gravy.

---

## Chilli Paneer

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/chilli-paneer/

Crispy paneer cubes stir-fried with peppers in a hot soy-chilli sauce — a vegetarian Indo-Chinese favourite.

**Cuisine:** Chinese · **Style:** Fried · **Protein:** Paneer
**Time:** 30 min total (15 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** dinner, snacks · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 250g paneer, cubed
- 2 tbsp cornflour
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp chilli sauce
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 1 onion, cubed
- 1 bell pepper, cubed
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, minced
- 2 tbsp oil
- Spring onions
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Coat paneer cubes in cornflour and salt.
2. Shallow fry until golden and crispy.
3. Heat oil in a wok, sauté garlic and ginger.
4. Add onion and bell pepper, stir fry.
5. Add all sauces and vinegar.
6. Toss in paneer and stir fry 2 minutes.
7. Garnish with spring onions. Serve dry or with gravy.

---

## Schezwan Noodles

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/schezwan-noodles/

Hakka noodles tossed in a fiery Schezwan sauce with vegetables — bold, spicy Indo-Chinese street food.

**Cuisine:** Chinese · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 4/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 200g hakka noodles
- 3 tbsp schezwan sauce
- 1 carrot, julienned
- 1/2 cup cabbage, shredded
- 1 bell pepper, julienned
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp vinegar
- 2 tbsp oil
- Spring onions

**Steps:**
1. Boil noodles, drain, toss with oil.
2. Heat oil in a wok, add garlic.
3. Stir fry vegetables for 2 minutes on high heat.
4. Add schezwan sauce, soy sauce, and vinegar.
5. Add noodles and toss well on high heat.
6. Garnish with spring onions. Serve hot.

---

## Momos

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/momos/

Steamed or fried dumplings filled with spiced chicken or vegetables — a hugely popular Tibetan-Indian street snack.

**Cuisine:** Chinese (Northeast India) · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 45 min total (30 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** snacks, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups maida
- 300g chicken mince
- 1 onion, finely chopped
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 1 inch ginger, minced
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp oil
- Salt and pepper to taste
- Chilli sauce for dipping

**Steps:**
1. Knead maida with water and salt. Rest 30 minutes.
2. Mix chicken mince with onion, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, oil, and pepper.
3. Roll dough thin, cut circles.
4. Place filling in center, pleat and seal.
5. Steam in a momo steamer for 12-15 minutes.
6. Serve hot with spicy tomato-chilli dipping sauce.

**Tips:**
- Seal edges well or the filling will leak during steaming.

---

## Pasta Arrabiata

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/pasta-arrabiata/

Penne in a fiery garlic and red chilli tomato sauce — simple Italian comfort with a spicy kick.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 25 min total (5 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegan · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 250g penne pasta
- 4 cloves garlic, sliced
- 1 can crushed tomatoes (400g)
- 1 tsp red chilli flakes
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- Fresh basil
- Parmesan (optional)
- Salt and pepper to taste

**Steps:**
1. Cook pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
2. Heat olive oil, sauté garlic until fragrant.
3. Add chilli flakes and crushed tomatoes.
4. Simmer 10 minutes until thickened.
5. Toss in pasta with a splash of pasta water.
6. Garnish with fresh basil and Parmesan.

---

## Grilled Chicken Salad

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/grilled-chicken-salad/

Juicy grilled chicken breast over mixed greens with a tangy vinaigrette — fresh, light, and protein-packed.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 27 min total (15 prep + 12 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 chicken breasts
- 4 cups mixed salad greens
- 1 cucumber, sliced
- 10 cherry tomatoes, halved
- 1/4 red onion, sliced
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- 1 tsp honey
- Salt and pepper to taste

**Steps:**
1. Season chicken with salt, pepper, and olive oil.
2. Grill on high heat for 5-6 minutes per side until cooked.
3. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
4. Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, mustard, and honey for dressing.
5. Toss greens with cucumber, tomatoes, and onion.
6. Top with sliced chicken and drizzle dressing.

---

## Mac & Cheese

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/mac-and-cheese/

Elbow macaroni baked in a creamy, gooey cheddar cheese sauce — the ultimate comfort food for all ages.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 35 min total (10 prep + 25 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 250g elbow macaroni
- 2 cups shredded cheddar cheese
- 2 tbsp butter
- 2 tbsp flour
- 2 cups milk
- 1/2 tsp mustard powder
- 1/4 tsp paprika
- Breadcrumbs for topping
- Salt and pepper to taste

**Steps:**
1. Cook macaroni until al dente. Drain.
2. Melt butter, whisk in flour to make a roux.
3. Gradually add milk, whisking constantly until thick.
4. Add cheese, mustard, paprika, salt, and pepper. Stir until melted.
5. Mix in macaroni, pour into a baking dish.
6. Top with breadcrumbs, bake at 190°C for 15 minutes until bubbly.

---

## Mushroom Risotto

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/mushroom-risotto/

Creamy Italian rice slowly stirred with mushrooms, white wine, and Parmesan — luxurious and deeply savoury.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 40 min total (10 prep + 30 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups arborio rice
- 200g mixed mushrooms, sliced
- 1 onion, finely diced
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- 1/2 cup white wine
- 4 cups warm vegetable stock
- 2 tbsp butter
- 1/4 cup Parmesan, grated
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Fresh thyme
- Salt and pepper

**Steps:**
1. Keep stock warm on low heat.
2. Sauté mushrooms in olive oil until golden. Set aside.
3. In the same pan, cook onion and garlic in butter.
4. Add rice, stir 1 minute until translucent at edges.
5. Add wine, stir until absorbed.
6. Add stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly, until rice is creamy.
7. Fold in mushrooms, Parmesan, and butter.
8. Serve immediately topped with thyme.

**Tips:**
- Patience is key — keep stirring and adding stock gradually.

---

## Pancakes

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/pancakes/

Fluffy, golden breakfast pancakes served with maple syrup and fresh berries — a weekend morning treat.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 25 min total (10 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg

**Ingredients:**
- 1.5 cups all-purpose flour
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup milk
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 2 tbsp melted butter
- 1 tsp baking powder
- 1/2 tsp vanilla extract
- Maple syrup
- Fresh berries
- A pinch of salt

**Steps:**
1. Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
2. In another bowl, whisk eggs, milk, butter, and vanilla.
3. Combine wet and dry ingredients. Do not overmix (some lumps are fine).
4. Heat a non-stick pan, pour 1/4 cup batter.
5. Cook until bubbles appear on surface, then flip.
6. Cook until golden. Stack and serve with syrup and berries.

---

## French Toast

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/french-toast/

Thick bread slices soaked in cinnamon-egg custard and pan-fried golden — a decadent breakfast classic.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** Sweet · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 15 min total (5 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** breakfast · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg

**Ingredients:**
- 4 thick slices bread
- 2 eggs
- 1/4 cup milk
- 1 tsp cinnamon
- 2 tbsp sugar
- 1 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 tbsp butter
- Honey or maple syrup
- Fresh fruits for topping

**Steps:**
1. Whisk eggs, milk, cinnamon, sugar, and vanilla.
2. Dip bread slices in the mixture, soak each side well.
3. Melt butter on a pan over medium heat.
4. Cook soaked bread until golden on both sides.
5. Serve topped with honey, fruits, and powdered sugar.

---

## Caesar Salad

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/caesar-salad/

Crisp romaine lettuce with creamy Caesar dressing, croutons, and Parmesan — the classic salad done right.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** Light · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 20 min total (15 prep + 5 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg

**Ingredients:**
- 1 head romaine lettuce, chopped
- 1/2 cup croutons
- 1/4 cup Parmesan shavings
- 2 tbsp mayonnaise
- 1 tbsp lemon juice
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 2 anchovies (optional)
- 1 tbsp olive oil
- Salt and pepper

**Steps:**
1. Make dressing: blend mayo, lemon juice, mustard, garlic, olive oil, salt, and pepper.
2. Wash and chop romaine lettuce.
3. Toast bread cubes in butter for croutons.
4. Toss lettuce with dressing.
5. Top with croutons and Parmesan shavings.
6. Serve immediately.

---

## Garlic Bread Pizza

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/garlic-bread-pizza/

Quick flatbread pizza loaded with garlic butter, mozzarella, and your favourite toppings — easy and crowd-pleasing.

**Cuisine:** Continental · **Style:** Baked · **Protein:** Veggies
**Time:** 30 min total (15 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** snacks, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian

**Ingredients:**
- 2 naan or flatbreads
- 4 tbsp garlic butter
- 1/2 cup pizza sauce
- 1 cup mozzarella, shredded
- 1 bell pepper, sliced
- 1/2 cup mushrooms, sliced
- 1/2 cup corn
- Dried oregano
- Chilli flakes
- Fresh basil

**Steps:**
1. Spread garlic butter on naan/flatbread.
2. Add a layer of pizza sauce.
3. Top with mozzarella and vegetables.
4. Sprinkle oregano and chilli flakes.
5. Bake at 200°C for 12-15 minutes until cheese is bubbly.
6. Garnish with fresh basil. Slice and serve.

---

## Pad Thai

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/pad-thai/

Stir-fried rice noodles with tamarind sauce, peanuts, and fresh lime — Thailand s most famous street dish.

**Cuisine:** Asian · **Style:** One Pot · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 25 min total (15 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 3 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 200g flat rice noodles
- 2 eggs
- 1 cup bean sprouts
- 3 tbsp tamarind paste
- 2 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
- 1 tbsp sugar
- 4 cloves garlic, minced
- 3 tbsp roasted peanuts, crushed
- 2 spring onions, chopped
- 1 lime
- 2 tbsp oil
- Red chilli flakes

**Steps:**
1. Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes. Drain.
2. Mix tamarind paste, soy sauce, and sugar for the sauce.
3. Heat oil in a wok, scramble eggs, set aside.
4. Sauté garlic, add noodles and sauce. Toss on high heat.
5. Add eggs and bean sprouts, toss briefly.
6. Serve with crushed peanuts, spring onions, lime, and chilli flakes.

---

## Thai Green Curry

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/thai-green-curry/

Creamy coconut curry with green chilli paste, vegetables, and basil — fragrant, spicy, and comforting.

**Cuisine:** Asian · **Style:** Curry · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 30 min total (10 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 400g chicken, cubed
- 1 can coconut milk (400ml)
- 3 tbsp green curry paste
- 1 zucchini, sliced
- 1/2 cup bamboo shoots
- 1 bell pepper, sliced
- 2 tbsp fish sauce
- 1 tbsp sugar
- Thai basil leaves
- 1 tbsp oil
- 2 kaffir lime leaves

**Steps:**
1. Heat oil, fry green curry paste for 1 minute.
2. Add half the coconut milk and cook until oil separates.
3. Add chicken and cook 5 minutes.
4. Add remaining coconut milk, vegetables, and kaffir lime leaves.
5. Add fish sauce and sugar. Simmer 10 minutes.
6. Stir in Thai basil. Serve with jasmine rice.

---

## Ramen

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/ramen/

Japanese noodle soup with a rich broth, soft-boiled egg, and toppings — warming and deeply satisfying.

**Cuisine:** Asian · **Style:** Soup · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Contains egg · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 200g ramen noodles
- 4 cups chicken stock
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp miso paste
- 1 tsp sesame oil
- 2 soft-boiled eggs
- 100g mushrooms, sliced
- 2 spring onions, sliced
- Nori sheets
- 1 tbsp ginger, grated
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- Chilli oil (optional)

**Steps:**
1. Simmer chicken stock with ginger, garlic, soy sauce, and miso.
2. Cook ramen noodles according to package. Drain.
3. Sauté mushrooms in sesame oil.
4. Place noodles in bowls, ladle hot broth over.
5. Top with halved soft-boiled egg, mushrooms, nori, and spring onions.
6. Drizzle chilli oil if desired. Serve immediately.

---

## Bibimbap

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/bibimbap/

Korean rice bowl topped with sautéed vegetables, a fried egg, and spicy gochujang sauce — colourful and balanced.

**Cuisine:** Asian · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 35 min total (20 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** medium · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 cups cooked rice
- 1 carrot, julienned
- 1 cup spinach
- 1 cup bean sprouts
- 1 zucchini, julienned
- 2 eggs
- 2 tbsp gochujang
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tsp sesame seeds
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Sauté each vegetable separately with a little oil and salt.
2. Blanch spinach, squeeze dry, season with sesame oil.
3. Fry eggs sunny side up.
4. Place rice in bowls, arrange vegetables in sections on top.
5. Top with fried egg and a generous dollop of gochujang.
6. Sprinkle sesame seeds. Mix everything together before eating.

---

## Egg Fried Rice

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/egg-fried-rice/

Simple wok-fried rice with fluffy scrambled eggs and spring onions — fast, delicious, and endlessly adaptable.

**Cuisine:** Asian · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Egg
**Time:** 15 min total (5 prep + 10 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 1/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Vegetarian · Contains egg · Gluten-free · Dairy-free

**Ingredients:**
- 3 cups cold cooked rice
- 3 eggs
- 3 cloves garlic, minced
- 2 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp sesame oil
- 3 spring onions, chopped
- White pepper
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Beat eggs with a pinch of salt.
2. Heat oil in a wok until smoking.
3. Scramble eggs quickly, break into pieces, set aside.
4. Add more oil, sauté garlic briefly.
5. Add cold rice, break up clumps, stir fry on high heat.
6. Add soy sauce, white pepper, and eggs. Toss well.
7. Finish with sesame oil and spring onions.

---

## Tacos

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/tacos/

Crunchy corn tortillas loaded with spiced chicken, salsa, and fresh toppings — fun, flavourful, and hands-on.

**Cuisine:** Mexican · **Style:** Bread Meal · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 30 min total (15 prep + 15 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 4 · **Spice:** 3/5
**Meal:** dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 400g chicken breast, shredded
- 8 taco shells
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- 1/2 tsp chilli powder
- 1 cup salsa
- 1/2 cup sour cream
- 1 cup shredded lettuce
- 1 tomato, diced
- 1/2 cup shredded cheese
- 1 lime
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Season chicken with cumin, paprika, chilli powder, and salt.
2. Cook in oil until done, then shred.
3. Warm taco shells in the oven.
4. Fill shells with chicken, lettuce, tomato, and cheese.
5. Top with salsa and sour cream.
6. Squeeze lime over. Serve immediately.

---

## Burrito Bowl

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/burrito-bowl/

A deconstructed burrito with rice, beans, grilled chicken, corn, and all the fixings — hearty and customisable.

**Cuisine:** Mexican · **Style:** Rice Bowl · **Protein:** Chicken
**Time:** 35 min total (15 prep + 20 cook) · **Difficulty:** easy · **Servings:** 2 · **Spice:** 2/5
**Meal:** lunch, dinner · **Dietary:** Non-Veg · Gluten-free

**Ingredients:**
- 2 chicken breasts
- 1.5 cups cooked rice
- 1 cup black beans
- 1/2 cup corn
- 1 avocado, sliced
- 1/2 cup salsa
- 1/4 cup sour cream
- 1 lime
- 1 tsp cumin
- 1 tsp paprika
- Shredded lettuce
- 2 tbsp oil
- Salt to taste

**Steps:**
1. Season chicken with cumin, paprika, and salt. Grill or pan-fry.
2. Slice grilled chicken.
3. Cook rice with a squeeze of lime.
4. Warm black beans and corn.
5. Assemble bowls: rice, beans, corn, chicken, lettuce.
6. Top with avocado, salsa, sour cream, and lime.

---

## Falafel Wrap

URL: https://itzshoaib.github.io/dinner-spinner/recipes/falafel-wrap/

Crispy chickpea fritters wrapped in warm pita with tahini, salad, and pickles — a satisfying Mediterranean meal.
